steps.title: Identify the genetic affinity evidence
steps.content: The passage acknowledges that tool similarities could result from imitation or trade, but reveals a key finding: Altınışık et al. discovered “genetic affinity among the populations” of these settlements—evidence that these populations were biologically connected.

steps.title: Apply genetic affinity to the answer choices
steps.content:
Genetic affinity indicates biological connection between populations, which explains their similar tools through population blending rather than requiring imitation or trade.
The other three either misuse genetic evidence to infer imitation patterns or migration order, or introduce unsupported assumptions about cultural differences.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
